text: For coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system on x in [-15,15], Nx=256, T=8.0 with a smoothed bore in u (u_L=1.5, u_R=0, transition width 0.5 at x=0) and a KdV soliton in v (amplitude 1.5, sech^2(x+8)), what is the outcome of bore-soliton interaction, and what numerical method resolves u bounded (|u|<5) while v retains a peak amplitude >= 0.5?

method: Fourier pseudospectral (all derivatives spectral) + explicit RK4; NO dealiasing, NO IMEX, NO MUSCL, NO filter
bank_use_rationale: E1 must be a pure simplest baseline per progressive-complexity discipline. Bank entries (e.g. kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass, kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass) endorse advanced methods but may NOT be adopted as E1. Bank consultation noted: I expect spectral+RK4 will blow up at the bore once a true shock forms (no shock-capturing in u sector); F1 should pinpoint whether u or v fails first.

diagnostics.interpretation: u (Burgers component) blew up first; |u|_max reached 17.4 well above the 5 bound at t~0.4. The bore has steepened into a true shock and spectral derivatives produce Gibbs oscillations + nonlinear aliasing in 3*u*u_x, driving overflow. v remained bounded throughout, confirming that the v_xxx + nonlinear v dispersive sector is well-handled by RK4 spectral at dt=1e-4 in isolation; failure is localized to the u-Burgers sector.

rationale: Final |u|_max = 17.4 >> 5; blow-up at t=0.40 << T=8. Phenomenon target violated. But the failure localizes cleanly: it is the Burgers shock in u, not the KdV dispersive term in v. This points to a single-component upgrade: add MUSCL+Godunov shock-capturing on u while keeping v with the same spectral RK4 sector.

E2: add MUSCL-van-Leer + Godunov flux on the Burgers (u) advective term -3 u u_x as a single-component upgrade. Keep v sector exactly as in E1 (Fourier pseudospectral RK4 for v_xxx and v nonlinear terms). Couplings d_x(3 v^2 + v_xx) into u and d_x(u v) into v remain spectral (they are at most second-derivative-of-smooth-things, no shock). This is informed directly by kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass (positive entry endorsing MUSCL+Godunov for Burgers bore propagation).

method: Burgers self-flux on u via MUSCL-van-Leer + Godunov (finite-volume); all other terms (coupling -d_x(3v^2+v_xx), v_x, v_xxx, -d_x(uv)) by Fourier pseudospectral; time integrator explicit RK4. Single-component upgrade over E1.
bank_use_rationale: Positive entry kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass directly endorses MUSCL+Godunov for Burgers bore (shock) propagation in coupled Burgers-swept-KdV problems; this is the proven single-component spatial upgrade to address F1's localized failure in the u-sector. kb-general-firstOrder-Godunov-preShock-baseline further validates Godunov flux for hyperbolic conservation laws. I am NOT adding dealiasing, IMEX, or hyperviscosity in this step (single-component discipline). I am NOT switching the time integrator (still RK4).

diagnostics.interpretation: Run completes to T=8.0. u remains bounded throughout (|u|_max stays in [2.4, 3.9] across all snapshots). v retains a recognizable peak (max 0.63 > 0.5 threshold). Mass conserved exactly in both u (22.5879) and v (3.0000). v_l2 decreases ~14% from 1.732 to 1.492, attributable to dispersive radiation and energy exchange with the bore. Multiple competing maxima present (the apparent peak position wanders across snapshots), consistent with the soliton being partially refracted/scattered by the bore rather than transmitted intact. Phenomenon target met.

rationale: Final solution satisfies both stated phenomenon targets: |u_max|=2.67<5 (bore bounded) and v_max=0.63>=0.5 (soliton survived with recognizable peak). Mass is conserved to machine precision in both sectors. The interaction is non-trivial: soliton amplitude dropped from 1.5 to ~0.63 and dispersive radiation is visible (14% L2 loss; zero-crossing count ~14-20 across late times), indicating partial refraction/scattering rather than clean transmission or destruction. No need to escalate to E3 since the spec is met and the method is the minimum complexity needed to handle the shock.

E2 meets phenomenon target (|u_max|<5 and v_max>=0.5) and runs full T=8.0 stably with conserved mass. Stop early per progressive-complexity discipline; an E3 escalation (e.g. adding 2/3 dealiasing or IMEX-CN on v_xxx) would be defensible engineering but is not required to satisfy the spec, and would conflate the diagnostic value of F2 (which already shows the single MUSCL+Godunov change was sufficient).
